video_title: Guy Dives Into a LEGENDARY Cheeseburger in NYC 🍔 | Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives | Food Network

dishes.description: Classic 70-30 beef patty, cooked on a super hot cast iron skillet, topped with American cheese and caramelized onions, served on a pedestrian bun.
dishes.guy_response: Crust of two billows from that screaming, smoking hot cast iron skillet. I like the juiciness of it. The cheese melting into it. The first time I bit in that burger, I was amazed. It tastes unlike any other burger in New York. I think it's really juicy and really excellent.

dishes.description: 8-ounce grass-fed brisket and chuck blend patty, topped with roasted bone marrow, raclette cheese, and a compound butter with rosemary, parsley, and roasted garlic, served on a brioche bun.
dishes.guy_response: Oh, man. Never tasted anything like this. The richness of the meat inside of it. Oh, yeah. Dynamite brioche bun, by the way. It has this very composed. Elegant dish profile from the herbaceousness of the butter. And there are clutcheese. Silky, creamy, a little tangy. It's beyond a burger's level. It's dynamite, man. Mid-rare bone marrow burger. It's the marrow all the way. It just melts in your mouth. It's wonderful. So juicy. The herbal butter kind of just like oozes through the meat. The bun is awesome. It's gluten-free. Wow. This is the place.

restaurants.name: Gastro Grind
restaurants.owner_chef: Greg and Belinda Rangel, Ruben Reyes
restaurants.guy_intro: We're here to check out a joint where a husband and wife and a stepson are doing burgers outside of the box. This is Gastro Grind.

dishes.description: High-quality ground patty topped with lobster, lemon aioli (with preserved lemons, garlic puree, Dijon), romesco sauce (with piquillo peppers, garlic puree, croutons, almonds, parsley), and garlic anchovy aioli, served on a potato bun with free-sale lettuce and crispy onions.
dishes.guy_response: There's so much big flavor in this. With the romesco, the brie, the anchovy aioli, then on top of it with the lobster, a very sophisticated burger. Your flavors all coincide together. They work together very well. Good texture to it, too. That's the real deal right there. That's the one.

dishes.description: Three patties made with ground croutons, raw diced onions, raw garlic, cumin, chili powder, and eggs, topped with provolone cheese, caramelized onions, sauteed mushrooms, house-made marinara sauce, and house-made pickles, served with hand-cut fries.
dishes.guy_response: Great meatball flavor. You can barely get your mouth around it, but it's really good. Holy moly. Look at that. Love the sauteed mushrooms. Nice little kiss of the marinara. That right there screaming Italian family Sunday dinner, that's ridiculously big. The sandwiches are like complete meals. It's got some southern kick to it, and it makes it totally awesome.

restaurants.name: Beer-Infused Burger Joint
restaurants.guy_intro: Everything's made in-house here. And they infuse their beers in a lot of their menu items. Even down to their not-so-basic burgers.

dishes.description: Sliders with herb goat cheese, house-made brioche buns, bacon jam (with bacon, shallots, brown sugar, red pepper flakes, honey, house-made beer), tri-blend patty (chuck, porter, brisket), roasted tomatoes, fresh arugula, and beer barbecue sauce (with garlic, onion, tomato paste, Worcestershire, brown sugar, yellow mustard, apple cider vinegar, plum tomato, blonde ale).
dishes.guy_response: The burger, burger, burger is really yummy. Probably one of the best burger sliders I've ever had. I love the goat cheese and the bacon jam. That's legit going down. It's not good unless you have to lick your fingers. What you put into one little burger is more than a lot of people put into their own menu. I mean, you've got such great flavors. The goat cheese is cool. It's tangy, good acid. You got a lot of sweet coming up from the bacon jam. Love the fat in the burger. The bun's delicious. It's nice. It's light. Flavor, design, attention to detail, creativity. Well done. Great burger. Burger, burger, burger up. Definitely. This is not your average Slider.
